# Debate Instructions

## COP31 Climate Negotiations

You are attending the **UNFCCC COP31**. The world has failed to meet its Paris targets. Temperatures are tracking above 2°C. Financing pledges remain unfulfilled. The pressure to produce a meaningful agreement has never been higher.

Your group represents one of **30 actors** in these negotiations. Your job is to use the JUSTICE IAM to quantify the consequences of different climate policies for your actor, and then to negotiate — hard — for the outcome your actor needs.

The debate is designed to prepare you for the **political reflection** part of your final assignment, in which you are asked to analyse how model-based knowledge is used, and misused, in political decision making.

---

---

## Actor assignment

The same groups as the final project will be used. **Participation is mandatory.**

1. Actor enrolment opens on Brightspace: **Collaboration → Group Self-Enrolment → Actor Assignment**
2. Only **one student per group** should register. Decide beforehand which actors your group prefers and designate one person to enrol.
3. Actors are assigned on a **first-come, first-served** basis. Waiting lists do not count — if you end up on a waiting list, choose another actor.
4. You have **24 hours** to enrol. After the deadline, enrolment closes and the actor your representative selected becomes your group's actor.

```{warning}
Your Brightspace group must already exist before enrolment opens. Actor mandates will be
distributed based on existing Brightspace groups.
```

---

## Confidentiality

Your mandate is **secret** and cannot be shared with other groups. You may communicate your intentions openly, but you cannot reveal or show your mandate document.
